name: Majorcan Midwife Toad
conservationEfforts: Discovered from fossil records in 1977, then found alive in 1980. Durrell Wildlife Conservation Trust captive breeding. Reintroduced to multiple gorge sites. Snake removal programs.
description: Known only from fossils until living populations were discovered in limestone gorges of Mallorca in 1980. Males carry strings of eggs wrapped around their hind legs until they hatch.
